ncs_standard_definition.description_short: Agribusiness Managers oversee the production, processing, and distribution of agricultural products. They develop and implement business strategies to maximize profits and minimize losses.
ncs_standard_definition.description_detailed: Agribusiness Managers work with farmers, suppliers, and other stakeholders to ensure the efficient production and distribution of agricultural products. They analyze market trends, manage budgets, and make strategic decisions to drive business growth.

first_stepper.description: High school students interested in agriculture and business can pursue a degree in agriculture, business, or a related field.

choice_maker.description: Individuals with a generic degree can pursue certifications or bridge courses in agriculture or business to transition into the field.

future_builder.description: Experienced professionals in the agriculture or business sector can transition into agribusiness management by developing advanced skills in areas like data analysis, marketing, and finance.
future_builder.prerequisite_experience: 2-3 years of experience in agriculture or business

problem_solver_uturn.description: Individuals from unrelated fields can transition into agribusiness management by developing relevant skills and building a portfolio of projects.
